title: Ctenitis biserialis
intro: Ctenitis biserialis is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae, commonly known as a type of Osmunda fern. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Ctenitis biserialis include flavonoids, triterpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make herbal preparations, such as infusions and decoctions, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Ctenitis biserialis is classified within the family Dryopteridaceae, which is a family of ferns commonly known as the wood ferns. This species is further classified as a member of the genus Ctenitis, which is a group of ferns native to tropical and subtropical regions. Ctenitis biserialis is an evergreen fern characterized by its distinctive, biserially arranged fronds, which are a key feature in its identification. The species is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, where it grows in shaded, humid areas. Ctenitis biserialis is also known as the 'biserial wood fern', reflecting its unique frond arrangement.
